title: Track ~/.zshrc as repo file zshrc with portability fixes
description:
Add /Users/christopherbartling/.dotfiles/zshrc with the current cleaned-up content from ~/.zshrc, fixing:
- Replace hardcoded /Users/christopherbartling/.docker/completions with $HOME/.docker/completions
- Append `[ -f "$HOME/.zshrc.local" ] && source "$HOME/.zshrc.local"` at the end
Repo-only — does NOT symlink yet. Verify diff against live ~/.zshrc only differs in those two ways. Verify zsh -n syntax check passes.

title: Stop loading agnoster theme and starship simultaneously
description: oh-my-zsh/core.sh sets ZSH_THEME="agnoster", and ~/.zshrc:55 then runs `eval "$(starship init zsh)"` which overrides the prompt entirely. Agnoster's setup is pure waste. Set ZSH_THEME="" (or remove the line) so only starship handles the prompt.

title: Fix double compinit and out-of-order fpath modifications
description: ~/.zshrc runs compinit twice: once via oh-my-zsh.sh (line 8) and again in the Docker completions block at the bottom. Additionally, fpath is modified AFTER compinit ran in two places (line 35 adds ~/.zsh/completion; the Docker block adds ~/.docker/completions then re-runs compinit). The correct pattern: do all fpath mutations first, then a single compinit. This is also a measurable startup-time fix.

title: Remove ssh-add and git config from shell startup
description: aliases/core.sh:6-7 runs `ssh-add -D && ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519` and `git config user.name/email` on every shell startup. This wipes the ssh-agent and re-prompts for the passphrase each new terminal, and the git config call sets repo-local config in whatever directory the shell opened in (almost never what you want). Remove these lines; rely on the on-demand `personal-git-setup` alias instead, or use `git config --global`.

title: Fix broken cat/bat alias quoting
description: aliases/core.sh:10 has `alias cat=bat --paging=never` which is broken — without quotes, --paging=never is parsed as an argument to the alias command itself, not part of the alias value. Should be: alias cat='bat --paging=never'

title: Add Tier 1 Claude Code workflow tools to Brewfile
description:
Add 6 high-leverage tools that improve day-to-day Claude Code workflows:

- yq (YAML query — the missing pair to jq)
- ast-grep (structural code search; better than rg for AST patterns)
- difftastic (structural diff; reviewing Claude PRs is the bottleneck)
- zoxide (frecency-based directory jumping; needs eval init in zshrc)
- tree (cheap directory layout for prompts)
- lazygit (TUI for reviewing/amending Claude commits)

Work:
1. Add entries to Brewfile in appropriate sections
2. brew install all 6 on this Mac
3. Add `eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"` to tracked zshrc after starship init
4. Verify zsh -n + startup time still ~150ms

title: Add bootstrap.sh end-to-end installer for fresh Mac
description:
Add /Users/christopherbartling/.dotfiles/bootstrap.sh with set -euo pipefail. Idempotent. Steps:
1. Detect macOS, ensure Xcode Command Line Tools (xcode-select --install if missing)
2. Install Homebrew if `command -v brew` fails
3. Clone repo to $HOME/.dotfiles if not running from inside it
4. brew bundle --file="$HOME/.dotfiles/Brewfile"
5. Install oh-my-zsh unattended with RUNZSH=no CHSH=no KEEP_ZSHRC=yes (so it doesn't overwrite the soon-to-be symlinked zshrc)
6. Install sdkman if ~/.sdkman missing (curl|bash)
7. mkdir -p ~/.nvm
8. Run scripts/macOS/link.zsh
9. Print next-steps block (gh auth login, nvm install --lts, etc.)

Test on this Mac as a no-op idempotency check before pushing.

title: Add scripts/macOS/link.zsh with backup-before-link safety
description:
Create scripts/macOS/link.zsh that symlinks:
- ~/.zshrc -> $DOTFILES/zshrc
- ~/.config/starship.toml -> $DOTFILES/configurations/starship.toml

Before linking, if the target exists and is not already a symlink to the right path, back it up to <file>.backup.<timestamp>. Idempotent — re-running should be a no-op. After this bead lands, run link.zsh on this Mac to switch ~/.zshrc to the symlink and verify startup time is still ~130ms.

title: Rewrite install_tools.zsh as brew bundle wrapper; fold fonts in
description: Collapse scripts/macOS/install_tools.zsh to a thin wrapper that runs `brew bundle --file=$DOTFILES/Brewfile` (preserving the existing entry point for muscle memory). Once fonts have been folded into the main Brewfile in bd-cqh, delete scripts/macOS/install_fonts_via_homebrew.zsh.

title: Add Brewfile (merged from brew bundle dump + install_tools.zsh)
description: Generate the canonical Brewfile by running brew bundle dump on this Mac, merging with the package list in scripts/macOS/install_tools.zsh, and hand-auditing. Must include zsh-syntax-highlighting and zsh-autosuggestions (recently installed but not yet tracked anywhere). Must include all current tap entries from install_tools.zsh. No behavior change in this bead — just adds the file at repo root.

title: Add history config, common setopts, and EDITOR
description:
Missing baseline zsh quality-of-life:

- No history config (default HISTSIZE/SAVEHIST are stingy). Add:
    HISTSIZE=50000
    SAVEHIST=50000
    HISTFILE=~/.zsh_history
    setopt SHARE_HISTORY HIST_IGNORE_ALL_DUPS HIST_IGNORE_SPACE HIST_REDUCE_BLANKS INC_APPEND_HISTORY EXTENDED_HISTORY

- No common setopts. Consider: AUTO_CD, EXTENDED_GLOB, NO_BEEP, INTERACTIVE_COMMENTS

- No EDITOR/VISUAL set. Some tools (git, less, cron) need it. Likely `export EDITOR='code --wait'`.

title: Consolidate PATH setup and delete dead boilerplate in zshrc
description:
PATH is currently set in 4 scattered places: paths/core.sh (~/bin), the PNPM_HOME block in the middle, ~/.local/bin at the bottom, plus whatever sdkman/nvm add. Consolidate to a single PATH section near the top using `typeset -U path` to dedupe.

Also delete the ~25 lines of dead oh-my-zsh installer boilerplate at the top of ~/.zshrc (bash PATH note, MANPATH/LANG/EDITOR/ARCHFLAGS commented templates, 'User configuration' header with no content) and the commented-out runtime sources (rbenv, pyenv, poetry).

Consider folding paths/core.sh and functions/core.sh inline into ~/.zshrc since each is one line / one function — less indirection.

title: Lazy-load nvm and sdkman to speed up shell startup
Remove .DS_Store from repo and ignore it
description: A .DS_Store file is committed at the repo root and is not in .gitignore. Add .DS_Store (and **/.DS_Store) to .gitignore and `git rm --cached .DS_Store`.

title: Resolve orphaned alias and function files
description: These alias files exist but are not loaded by aliases/core.sh: aliases/aws.sh, aliases/cleanchoice-energy.sh, aliases/costco.sh, aliases/costco-browse.sh, aliases/localstack-experiments.sh. Also functions/cleanchoice.sh is not referenced anywhere. Either wire each into core.sh as a loader alias, or delete.

title: Starship: drop pyenv_version_name from [python] module
description: configurations/starship.toml has `pyenv_version_name = true` in the [python] module. This triggers a `pyenv version-name` shellout per prompt render. Since you're moving toward uv (and we already consolidated runtimes/pyenv.sh to no-op when pyenv isn't installed), this option is stale. Remove it. Tiny perf savings, removes a stale config knob.

title: Starship: add [azure] module for symmetry with aws/gcloud
description:
We added [aws] and [gcloud] in dotfiles-0bo but skipped [azure]. You have azure-cli installed and use it (azure-experiments aliases). Same safety argument: knowing the active subscription before destructive commands.

Suggested config:
[azure]
disabled = false
format = 'on [$symbol($subscription)]($style) '
symbol = "az "
style = "blue bold"

title: Starship: add [git_state] module
description:
Add the [git_state] module so the prompt shows when git is mid-operation: REBASING (3/7), MERGING, CHERRY-PICKING, BISECTING. Saves you from getting confused mid-rebase.

Suggested config:
[git_state]
format = '\([$state( $progress_current/$progress_total)]($style)\) '
cherry_pick = "[🍒 PICKING](bold red)"

title: Starship: add [direnv] module
description:
You have direnv installed but no prompt indicator. Add a [direnv] module so when an .envrc is loaded the prompt shows it (e.g. "📂 loaded/allowed"). Useful because direnv silently mutates your environment when you cd into a project.

Suggested config:
[direnv]
disabled = false
symbol = "📂 "
format = "[$symbol$loaded/$allowed]($style) "

title: Tweak starship: disable kubernetes, add aws/gcloud, truncate_to_repo
description:
Three focused changes to configurations/starship.toml:

1. Disable [kubernetes] module — perf win, it shells out on every prompt render even when no context is active.
2. Add [aws] and [gcloud] modules so the active profile/account is visible before destructive commands.
3. Set truncate_to_repo = true in [directory] for cleaner paths inside git repos.

Verify zsh startup time is unchanged (or better) after the changes.

title: Document all Homebrew packages in PACKAGES.md
description:
Add a markdown file at the repo root that documents every package in Brewfile and Brewfile.k8s. Format:
- One table per Brewfile section (Core CLI, Modern CLI, Dev tooling, etc.)
- Columns: Package | Description | Source / homepage
- Link to public source repo when available; fall back to homepage for proprietary tools
- Cover brews and casks. Briefly mention vscode/cargo entries are also tracked but don't document each.
- Include Brewfile.k8s in its own section.

title: Add Tier 2 Claude Code workflow tools to Brewfile
description:
Add 6 second-tier tools that pair well with Claude Code workflows:

- tokei (fast code stats; useful for planning sessions)
- glow (terminal markdown renderer for Claude-generated reports/plans)
- atuin (better shell history with sync across the 3 Macs; do NOT auto-init in zshrc — opt-in only)
- poppler (pdftotext etc; escape hatch for PDFs the read tool can't handle)
- imagemagick (image preprocessing to cut token cost on screenshots)
- gitleaks (specialized secret scanner; safety net for what Claude produces)

Work:
1. Add entries to Brewfile
2. brew install all 6 on this Mac
3. Do NOT add atuin init to zshrc — leave that as an opt-in choice
4. Verify Brewfile parses + zsh startup unchanged

title: Rewrite README with bootstrap and migration runbook
description:
Replace the manual ~/.zshrc snippet section in README.md with:
- One-line bootstrap instructions for a fresh Mac (clone + run bootstrap.sh)
- A 'Syncing an existing Mac' runbook (git pull, backup, run bootstrap, verify)
- Documentation of the ~/.zshrc.local escape hatch
- Brief notes on Brewfile and how to keep it updated

title: Add .zshrc.local.example and gitignore .zshrc.local
description: Add a short .zshrc.local.example file at repo root documenting the per-machine override pattern (the new tracked zshrc sources $HOME/.zshrc.local at the end if present). Add .zshrc.local to .gitignore so an actual local file on each Mac is never committed.

title: Add Brewfile.k8s and rewrite install_k8s_tools.zsh as wrapper
description: Move the k8s package list from scripts/macOS/install_k8s_tools.zsh into a new Brewfile.k8s at repo root (kept separate from main Brewfile because not every Mac needs k8s). Rewrite the script as a brew bundle wrapper.

title: Resolve duplicate runtime files and fix rbenv quoting bug
description:
Cleanup in runtimes/:

- nvm.sh and nvm-homebrew.sh are duplicates that only differ in install path. Pick one (homebrew is the active one) and delete the other.

- pyenv.sh and pyenv-ubuntu.sh are inconsistent setups for the same tool. pyenv.sh only puts shims on PATH and skips `pyenv init`, so virtualenvs and `pyenv shell` won't work. pyenv-ubuntu.sh runs `pyenv init --path` properly. Consolidate to one cross-platform file.

- rbenv.sh:4 has a quoting bug: `export PATH="~/.rbenv/shims:$PATH"` — `~` does not expand inside double quotes, so this puts a literal `~/.rbenv/shims` on PATH. Use $HOME or unquoted ~.

title: Install zsh-syntax-highlighting and zsh-autosuggestions
description:
Two of the highest-leverage zsh UX upgrades and currently neither is installed.

  brew install zsh-syntax-highlighting zsh-autosuggestions

Source from /opt/homebrew/share/... in ~/.zshrc. IMPORTANT: zsh-syntax-highlighting must be sourced LAST (after everything else, including any plugin frameworks) so its highlighter wraps every command.

title: Refresh README .zshrc snippet to match reality
description: README.md's .zshrc snippet doesn't mention paths/, runtimes/claude.sh, or ai-tools/, so anyone (including future-you on a new machine) following it gets a partial setup. Update the snippet to reflect what's actually needed.
notes: Findings from dotfiles-q9c: ~/.zshrc actually sources runtimes/claude.sh and runtimes/sdkman.sh, but the README snippet has sdkman commented out and doesn't mention claude.sh at all. README should reflect this.

title: Verify paths/golang.sh and runtimes/claude.sh are sourced
description: paths/golang.sh and runtimes/claude.sh are not referenced in the README .zshrc snippet. Verify whether the user's actual ~/.zshrc sources them; if not, they're dead code and should be deleted (or the README should be updated to source them).

title: Collapse duplicate ssh/git identity aliases
description: aliases/core.sh defines two aliases for the same thing: git-set-ssh-key (line 26) ≡ personal-add-ssh-key (line 31), and git-set-config (line 27) ≡ personal-git-config (line 32). Pick one naming scheme and delete the others.

title: Starship: review and disable unused language modules
description:
configurations/starship.toml currently has [java], [nodejs], [rust], [python], [docker_context]. Each adds a small detection cost when relevant files exist in the cwd. Audit and disable any you don't actively use.

Recommendation: leave them all enabled — having context the moment you do touch a language is worth the microseconds. But this bead exists if you decide you want a leaner prompt.

Easy to do: `disabled = true` per module section.

title: Starship: add [hostname] and [username] modules for SSH safety
description:
Currently configurations/starship.toml has neither [hostname] nor [username]. If you SSH between Macs (or to remote boxes) it helps to know which one you're on. Default-hide them locally and only show on SSH.

Suggested:
[hostname]
ssh_only = true
format = '[$ssh_symbol$hostname](bold red) in '

[username]
style_user = "yellow bold"
style_root = "red bold"
format = "user: [$user]($style) "
show_always = false

Only matters if you actually SSH between your three Macs. If you don't, skip.

title: Starship: enable [time] module
description:
[time] is currently commented out in configurations/starship.toml. Enabling adds a timestamp to the prompt — useful for debugging "what was I doing at 2pm yesterday" via shell history correlation.

Suggested:
[time]
disabled = false
format = "🕒 [%T] "
style = "dimmed white"

Pairs well with enabling right_format (dotfiles-a2z) so the timestamp lives on the right side of the prompt.

title: Starship: add yellow battery warning at 50%
description:
[battery] currently has only one display rule (red below 20%). Add a yellow heads-up at 50% so you get warning before it's urgent.

Add to configurations/starship.toml:
[[battery.display]]
threshold = 50
style = "bold yellow"

title: Starship: add right_format for time or cmd_duration
description:
Right now configurations/starship.toml uses only the left side of the prompt (the right_format line is commented out). You could move [time] or [cmd_duration] to the right side so they don't push the typing line further down on each invocation.

Suggested:
right_format = """$cmd_duration"""

Pairs nicely with enabling [time] (dotfiles-ahf — separate bead).

title: Starship: try alternative [character] symbols
description:
Current [character] uses ➜ (the agnoster classic). Pure preference, but worth experimenting with:

- ❯ — minimal, common starship default
- λ — functional aesthetic
- Two-tone: success_symbol = "[❯](bold green)[❯](bold cyan)"
- ▶ — simple triangle

No functional impact. Pick whichever you like best, or leave as-is.

title: Starship: define explicit top-level format
description:
Currently configurations/starship.toml has no top-level `format = """..."""` so it relies on starship's default module ordering. Defining one explicitly lets you:

- Control which modules appear and in what order
- Remove modules entirely from the left prompt without disabling them (move to right_format)
- Pin the order so future starship updates don't shuffle things

Most invasive change in this batch. Worth doing if you want fine-grained layout control. Skip if you're happy with defaults.

title: Starship: make [git_status] symbols visually consistent
description:
configurations/starship.toml's [git_status] mixes emoji (📦, ⚔️) with text (+${count}, ??, ✎, »). It's choppy. Pick one style:

(a) All-icon — use Nerd Font glyphs since you have nerd fonts installed (nf-fa-* etc.)
(b) All-text — drop the emoji, use plain ASCII like ! ? +N -N >M

Aesthetic only, no functional impact.

title: Tier 3: situational Claude Code tools (deferred)
description:
Deferred. These four tools are useful for specific Claude Code workflows but should only be installed when the use case actually arises:

- miller (mlr) — "jq for CSV/TSV". Install when you need to clean CSV exports.
- grpcurl — gRPC testing. Install if any project starts using gRPC.
- pgcli — Postgres CLI with autocompletion. Install when working on a Postgres project (none currently).
- ollama (cask) — local LLM runner. Install if you want to pair Claude with local models for proprietary code review.

When ready to install one, edit Brewfile, brew install, close this bead (or split per tool).

title: Add scripts/macOS/doctor.zsh for drift detection
description:
Optional: add a doctor.zsh script that runs:
- brew bundle check --file=$DOTFILES/Brewfile (reports missing packages)
- Verifies ~/.zshrc and ~/.config/starship.toml symlinks point at the repo
- Reports any drift
Useful to run after git pull on secondary Macs to verify sync.

title: Make per-project alias files silent on load
description: Each per-project alias file (e.g. aliases/lifetime.sh) prints a 30-line menu of available aliases when sourced. This is intrusive when the file is auto-loaded by the chpwd hook (dotfiles-dtn). Refactor each to move the menu echo into a separate `<prefix>-help` function that prints on demand, leaving the source itself silent.

title: Migrate remaining project aliases to chpwd auto-load mapping
description: functions/project-aliases.sh now auto-loads project aliases via a chpwd hook, but only 3 entries are seeded in PROJECT_ALIAS_MAP (lifetime, acme-inc-2026, wici). The remaining ~27 alias files in aliases/ still rely on the old manual loader pattern. For each one, determine the project's directory under ~/github-sandbox/ and add a PROJECT_ALIAS_MAP entry, then remove the corresponding loader alias from aliases/core.sh. Some files may be for non-active projects and can be deleted instead.

title: Replace per-project alias loaders with chpwd hook or direnv
description:
There are 30 per-project alias-loader aliases (e.g., `lifetime-aliases`, `acme-inc-2026-aliases`). To use a project's aliases, you must remember to type the loader, which then re-prints a 30-line menu every time. Better patterns:

(A) Auto-load via chpwd hook: when you cd into ~/github-sandbox/lifetime, source aliases/lifetime.sh automatically.

(B) Use direnv: drop a .envrc in each project that does `source ~/.dotfiles/aliases/<project>.sh`. direnv handles load/unload.

Either eliminates the 'did I remember to load aliases' friction. Side task: move the menu echos from the alias files into a separate `<prefix>-help` function so loading is silent.

Larger scope — could split into separate beads for the mechanism choice and the per-file menu refactor.

title: Sort the alias loader block in core.sh
description: The 'Specific alias loaders' block in aliases/core.sh (lines 41-71) is partly alphabetized and partly not. Sort it consistently so future additions are obvious.

title: Trim oh-my-zsh/core.sh boilerplate comments
description: oh-my-zsh/core.sh is mostly the upstream oh-my-zsh template comments. Trim to the ~3 lines that actually do anything (ZSH_THEME and plugins=).

title: Delete commented-out ll alias leftover
description: aliases/core.sh:11 has a commented-out `#alias ll='eza -l --icons --no-user --group-directories-first --time-style long-iso'` directly above the active definition. Just delete the commented line.

title: Decide whether .fleet/settings.json belongs in dotfiles
description: .fleet/settings.json is JetBrains Fleet IDE-local config. Decide whether this should live in a public dotfiles repo or be removed/ignored.
